terms.definition: A scalar order parameter summarizing the alignment of social actors with shared norms
A macroscopic variable that distinguishes phases of a system, being near zero in one phase and finite in another
Aggregate strength of institutional, legal, and normative limits on individual behavior
A discontinuous change in the state of a system when a control parameter crosses a critical threshold
An unobserved construct inferred statistically from multiple observed indicators
A measure of linear association between two variables, ranging from -1 to 1
A transformation converting Pearson r to a normally distributed statistic for significance testing
Probability of observing data as extreme as the sample assuming the null hypothesis is true
A point in a time series where statistical properties change, indicating a regime shift
System dynamics exhibiting a sharp change once a variable crosses a critical point
A comparison population not subjected to the experimental condition, used to isolate causal effects
A Christian tradition emphasizing adult baptism and communal simplicity, exemplified by the Amish
A narrow temporal span during which the system undergoes its fastest change
The specific value of constraint pressure below which coherence collapses
A phase transition involving discontinuity in the first derivative of free energy, showing latent heat
An econometric procedure for locating multiple structural breaks in linear models
The cumulative set of events that lower constraint pressure over time
The baseline expectation that most people in society can be trusted
Rate of change of coherence over time
Simultaneous timing of regime shifts across multiple domains
